Craft.Name: Venom Distillation and Alchemy
Craft.DistinctiveProperty: Extraction of jungle toxins into stable, potent essences used for medicine, dyes, and trade
Craft.MasterMarker: Can synthesize antitoxins from venoms of competing species — a sign of deep understanding
Craft.FailureCondition: active practitioners below 2 for more than one cycle; all venom stores depleted

RootConflict.Adversary: House Vex's own greed and external pressure
RootConflict.AdversaryProject: A weapons merchant (Kess Ballow) forcing the family to distill their finest toxins into assassination compounds
RootConflict.SecretTruth: The founder's original intent was healing; the venom trade began as medicine but is being weaponized by external demand
RootConflict.DisclosureStakes: Exposing Kess's weapons operation brings legal trouble but restores the family's original mission; complying means slow corruption into a kill-for-hire operation

NpcRoles.Wants: For jungle venom to heal the sick, not harm the innocent
NpcRoles.Fears: That his life's work would become a tool of assassination
NpcRoles.SelfDeception: Believed the jungle itself would prevent misuse — that dangerous knowledge would naturally stay contained

NpcRoles.Fears: The needles and fangs in the distillery
NpcRoles.SelfDeception: Thought the bottles she opened were safe — all marked with symbols
NpcRoles.ChangeCondition: N/A — dead (died when she opened a mislabeled venom bottle three years ago)

NpcRoles.Wants: To honor Vorus's original vision and never repeat Lyra's tragedy
NpcRoles.Fears: That refusing Kess will bring retaliation; that accepting will doom them anyway
NpcRoles.SelfDeception: Tells himself he can keep the family safe by meeting Kess's demands while secretly preparing to refuse
NpcRoles.ChangeCondition: Realizes that Kess's patience is finite — a choice must be made before the next monsoon

NpcRoles.Wants: To make the family wealthy and respected in the trade
NpcRoles.Fears: Being stuck in a jungle village forever, making medicine no one values
NpcRoles.SelfDeception: Believes Kess's poisons are 'legitimate commerce' — high-value work in a cutthroat market
NpcRoles.ChangeCondition: Confronted with proof that Kess uses the toxins to kill innocents; offered a competing buyer who values healing compounds at equal price

NpcRoles.Wants: The finest poisons the jungle can offer — a reliable supply chain for a growing assassination network
NpcRoles.Fears: Losing House Vex to moral squeamishness; a competing poison house undercutting his prices
NpcRoles.SelfDeception: Tells himself the people he hires to use the poisons made their own choices — he's just a merchant
NpcRoles.ChangeCondition: Competing poison merchant (Valdris) approaches House Vex with a higher offer for healing compounds; Theron accepts
NpcRoles.AntagonistMotivation: Profit maximization dressed as business necessity
NpcRoles.AntagonistEmotionalStyle: Charming to business partners, coldly transactional otherwise
NpcRoles.AntagonistVulnerability: Genuinely afraid of the jungle itself — relies on House Vex to filter its dangers into bottles
NpcRoles.AntagonistLineWontCross: Will not personally enter the distillery or jungle; his fear is too great
NpcRoles.ChangedByCondition: Offered a monopoly on Valdris's healing poisons (redirected to legitimate medical channels) at the same profit margin Kess currently demands

NpcRoles.Wants: To live peacefully with her choices and feed her family
NpcRoles.Fears: That speaking out will cost her her position and her family's security
NpcRoles.SelfDeception: Tells herself 'the poisons go out anyway; she's just a courier, not responsible for how they're used'
NpcRoles.ChangeCondition: Player demonstrates that Kess's customers include warlords known to target villages — makes her fear of complicity concrete

DangerousDisclosures.Condition: If kess_assassination_use revealed to Kess before the SuccessionHinge resolves
DangerousDisclosures.Consequence: Kess burns the founder's letter and moves his operation to a different house; the chance to turn him is lost

DangerousDisclosures.Condition: If competing_merchant_valdris revealed to Nyla before the SuccessionHinge resolves
DangerousDisclosures.Consequence: Nyla contacts Valdris independently, negotiates a split supply arrangement, and locks Theron out of the decision

PressurePoints.UrgencyTrigger: Kess demands the entire monsoon harvest early, forcing the family to strip the jungle of specimens
PressurePoints.UrgencyEffect: Closes the Restored path; locks in TransformedA as the best achievable outcome

Paths.EmotionalCostDescription: Refuse Kess's demand; accept the risk of losing the contract
Strip the jungle to meet Kess's demand; short-term profit, long-term depletion

PressurePoints.UrgencyTrigger: Nyla negotiates directly with Kess for a personal profit-sharing arrangement
PressurePoints.UrgencyEffect: Nyla becomes Kess's advocate; Theron's authority fractures

TransitionRules.Condition: Nyla is confronted with proof of Kess's assassination operations AND offered a competing buyer at equal profit
UrgencyTrigger fires before Nyla has this confrontation

Paths.EmotionalCostDescription: Nyla realizes the poisons she's helped distill are used to kill; grief and shame follow
Nyla commits fully to the high-profit path; Theron loses his ally

PressurePoints.UrgencyTrigger: Theron steps down; Nyla assumes full control and immediately renegotiates with Kess
PressurePoints.UrgencyEffect: Corrupted becomes the most likely outcome; Restored closes

TransitionRules.Condition: Theron makes a final choice about Kess's arrangement after all evidence is known

Paths.EmotionalCostDescription: Theron names Nyla co-leader only if she accepts the healing mission; Nyla must abandon profit maximization
Theron keeps Kess's account but reroutes profits to medicine; the family survives on blood money
Theron cuts ties with Kess; the family loses 70% of income but becomes morally clean

PressurePoints.UrgencyTrigger: Kess discovers the founder's letter; burns it to remove evidence of the original healing intent
PressurePoints.UrgencyEffect: Evidence of Vorus's true mission is lost; TransformedB and Corrupted become the only viable paths

TransitionRules.Condition: Player reads Vorus's original letter (hidden in the distillery)
Kess is confronted about his use before the letter is found

HiddenEvidence.GateCondition: Locked in Theron's study; key is either obtained from Theron directly or hidden in the original distillery stones

RevealMethods.Method: Ask Theron directly; he will share if he trusts the player's intent
Search the distillery for the loose stone Vorus carved the letter into

Paths.EmotionalCostDescription: Reading Vorus's own words about the healing mission is bittersweet; it clarifies the family's duty
The letter is destroyed; only profit-driven memory remains

Interactions.Description: Place a vial of healing salve in the vial holder — continue his work to cure the sick
Open the journal to the section on universal antitoxins — his gift to healers everywhere
Hang her cloak in the distillery entrance — a reminder of the price of carelessness
Plant a monsoon lily at the distillery threshold — where joy and danger meet

EndStates.Name: The Healer's Path
EndStates.ExternalResonanceDescription: House Vex becomes known across the basin as purveyors of genuine healing medicines; Kess's operation crumbles without their supply

EndStates.Name: The Reluctant Compromise
EndStates.ExternalResonanceDescription: House Vex survives as a smaller, struggling healing house; they lose prestige but maintain honor

EndStates.Name: The Profitable Shadow
EndStates.ExternalResonanceDescription: House Vex grows wealthier but earns whispered condemnation; insiders know what they've become

EndStates.Name: The Severed Thread
EndStates.ExternalResonanceDescription: House Vex collapses financially; Theron passes leadership to an outsider; the craft dies with the generation

EndStates.Name: The Assassin's Arsenal
EndStates.ExternalResonanceDescription: House Vex becomes a subsidiary of Kess's assassination network; jungle venom becomes a tool of empire

CorruptionGradient.EarlyWarningSignal: Kess delivers his first assassination contract request directly to Nyla, bypassing Theron
CorruptionGradient.PointOfNoReturn: CompromisedSustenance path taken in PoisonMerchant while Overharvest is already resolved
